memory.summary: The smell from my roommate's laundry is killing my focus, so I'm heading to the library immediately. No time to nag; I just need to get this work done before my energy dips.
Escaping the smell to the library saved my focus, but ignoring the roommate means tonight's sleep debt will only grow worse.
Progress stalled at 48% after two hours; my fatigue is clearly slowing me down. I need to push harder now or risk missing the deadline.
I pushed through the fatigue and hit 62% progress, proving I can force results even when drained. The deadline is closer, so I won't stop until this is done.
I pushed the reading response to 73% despite my low energy, but ignoring roommate_R's return guarantees another night of poor sleep and growing stress.
I squeezed out another 4% despite hitting rock bottom on energy, proving I can grind through fatigue. But this relentless pace is burning me out and guaranteeing tomorrow will be a disaster.
I dragged myself to 81% despite hitting zero energy, proving I can force output even when completely drained. This relentless grinding is burning me out, but the grade matters more than my health.
I forced another 6% despite zero energy, but the room issue guarantees poor sleep and growing debt. The grade matters more than my health.
Directly addressing the odor worked instantly, saving me from a messy conflict. Now I can finally focus on my work without that distraction.
I pushed through to 93% despite zero energy and rising sleep debt, proving the grade is worth my health. I'm drained but that progress is what actually matters.
Another 2% grind with zero energy proves the grade outweighs my health, even as sleep debt hits four hours.
Another 1% push at zero energy confirms the grade is worth my health, even as sleep debt hits four hours.
